title: Resolution integrity and failure-provenance hardening
outcome: E09.S4.1 closes the reviewed S4 integrity gaps: every resolution error carries one safe typed partial trace; the versioned digest binds the complete public command plus canonical executable/cwd/manifest identities, selection provenance, and diagnostics; authored routing parse failures fail closed; structured entrypoint fields match the closed schema; manifest authorization uses exact canonical identity; ordered PATH remains first-hit while distinct Panoply projections return executable_ambiguous; and ResolveSuccess exposes the immutable profile/policy handoff required by S5. No policy decision, spawn path, terminal-provider control, state-home creation, or RuntimeCommandRecord persistence was added.
validation: PASS: Takogami semantic check; 37 library + 17 CLI smoke + 17 contract + 27 resolution CLI tests (98 total); lint and formatting checks; Ontarch sync/validate including routing-schema parity; Panoply generation and substrate validation.
decisions: Keep SCHEMA_VERSION 0.1.0; failure provenance and executable-selection evidence are additive explanation data.
Represent completed resolution steps with a closed snake-case enum and keep success/failure explanation types distinct.
Hash reversible platform-native path encodings rather than display strings; keep canonical paths private.
Normalize set-like resolved fields before sealing while preserving argv order exactly.
Use first-hit semantics for ordered deduplicated PATH input; reserve executable_ambiguous for unordered distinct Panoply candidates.
Fail closed on malformed requested or unknown-identity authored descriptors without emitting descriptor contents or external absolute paths.
Do not implement policy evaluation or an executor while preparing an immutable PolicyEvaluationInput handoff.
handoff: E09.S5 should consume ResolveSuccess::policy_evaluation_input and evaluate the requested operation plus the same immutable digest-bound child plan. It must not reload descriptors, repeat manifest/executable lookup, rebuild argv, or mutate the sealed plan.
